risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Treatment allocation used a minimization algorithm with a random component [8,10]." Comment: This explicitly describes a random allocation method. Minimization with a random component is an acceptable random sequence generation approach under Cochrane guidance, so the allocation sequence was likely generated using a proper random process.

Quote: "Treatment allocation used a minimization algorithm with a random component [8,10]." Comment: This describes the allocation/randomization method, not how the upcoming assignment was concealed from the person enrolling participants. The report does not explicitly mention a concealment mechanism such as central randomization, pharmacy-controlled allocation, or sequentially numbered opaque sealed envelopes; therefore allocation concealment is unclear.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The TTrials are a coordinated group of multi-center, double-blind placebo-controlled trials..." and "Participants received AndroGel®, testosterone in an alcohol-water gel, or matched placebo gel, administered transdermally daily, for one year." and "The testosterone dose was adjusted by the (unblinded) data coordinating center as needed to maintain testosterone concentration levels between 500 and 800 ng/ml; similar adjustments were performed on matched placebo participants to maintain blinding." Comment: The report explicitly states the trial was double-blind, used a matched placebo gel, and describes dose adjustments in both groups specifically to maintain blinding. Although the data coordinating center was unblinded, this does not indicate that participants or treating personnel were unblinded. The dominant outcomes in this study are objective imaging/laboratory outcomes, which are unlikely to be influenced by any residual knowledge of allocation.

Quote: "Experienced readers in the CCTA core lab blinded to the assigned treatment visually evaluated the CCTA scans for the presence, extent, and severity of coronary artery plaque." and "The baseline and follow up scans were compared side by side (with blinding to both treatment arm and date of study [baseline vs. 12 month] to ensure like segments were compared and measured." and "The CCTA-core lab read all CTA scans, blinded to all demographics, testosterone randomization and risk factors..." Comment: Outcomes were assessed by independent/blinded imaging readers in a core laboratory. The measured outcomes in this report are imaging/lab-type CCTA endpoints (non-calcified plaque volume, total plaque volume, CAC score, subcutaneous fat), which are largely objective and standardized, although some reader input/manual correction was involved. Because assessors were explicitly blinded and the outcomes are not patient-reported subjective measures in this study report, detection bias is low.

Quote: "All participants still enrolled at 12 months with an eGFR > 60 and no new contraindication had a second CCTA scan to repeat the same measures obtained at the baseline visit." and "All subjects with a baseline and 12-month plaque volume measurement will be included in the analysis." Also, "One hundred sixty-five men were entered into the Cardiovascular Trial and had a baseline CCTA." Comment: The paper reports the number with baseline scans (165) but does not report how many participants in each randomized arm had 12-month CCTA/plaque measurements, nor per-arm losses/reasons for missing outcome data at 12 months. Because post-randomization analysed denominators for the outcome are not provided, attrition on the analysed outcome cannot be quantified; per guidance this is Unclear rather than High.
